winner_kind: patch (per-(N,K) Triton config JSON dropped into aiter/ops/triton/configs/gemm/)
provenance_detail: unittest.py sha256 == meta.unittest_sha256 (de9e80c2...); synthesized GEMM op, no reference_io.pt, meta shapes/dtype unchanged.
isolated_detail: in-process A/B (config/verify_isolated.py) on GPU0: prefill geomean 1.1023x (M=8192:1.095, 15360:1.099, 16384:1.113); decode M=1/64/1024 stay generic BM=128 -> 1.00x (no regression). cross_relerr=0.0 at every M (bit-identical, tuned config = same kernel, different tiling).
engagement_detail: After deploying the JSON, get_gemm_config('GEMM-A8W8_BLOCKSCALE', M, 34816, 5120) returns is_tuned=True and BM=256/nw=8/GROUP_M=4 for prefill M (8192/15360/16384), generic BM=128 for decode M (1/64/1024). Before deploy: is_tuned=False generic fallback. Live sglang GEMM path (aiter_w8a8_block_fp8_linear -> aiter triton gemm_a8w8_blockscale -> _get_config(M,N,K)) uses this exact loader, so the tuned config is actually served.
e2e_protocol: tight 2-launch A/B on GPU0, TP=1, mem-fraction 0.85, --attention-backend triton, REPEATS=2, port 31000, ISL/OSL/conc 1024/1024/64. Equal mem-fraction both legs (config-JSON adds no weight cache -> identical KV budget).
parity_detail: kernel output bit-identical to stock (cross_relerr=0.0); no numeric change vs accepted server.
reason: Engaged + parity-safe but a real e2e regression: cand_med 951.077 < ref_med 953.526 => delta -0.257% (non-overlapping, on the wrong side). Per do-no-harm three-verdict rule, never stack a regression. Expected Amdahl outcome: the +1.10x prefill GEMM at N=34816,K=5120 (57.2% of captured prefill-window GPU) does NOT convert to steady-state output tok/s at the 1024/1024/conc=64 workload, which is decode/TPOT-bound; decode Ms (1/64) deliberately stay on the generic BM=128 config (BM=256 wastes lanes on tiny M), so the tuned prefill tiles never touch the throughput-critical regime. Matches the MEMORY note: this shape's config-sweep direction is a ~1.0x lever, not a win.
